title: Delicate Egyptian Couscous Casserole
description: Buttery and crumbly, this Egyptian couscous casserole pairs char-grilled bird's eye chillies with fragrant cumin and smoked paprika for a romantic bite.
instructions: Char the bird's eye chillies over an open flame until blackened, then peel and roughly chop, setting aside.
In a saucepan, bring vegetable broth to a gentle simmer, then remove from heat and stir in couscous, cover, and let steam for 5 minutes.
Stir in melted butter, ground cumin, smoked paprika, chopped chillies, lemon zest, olive oil, salt, and pepper, mixing until the couscous is evenly coated.
Fold in chopped parsley, then transfer the mixture into four pre-lined mini cocottes, packing firmly.
Place the cocottes on a baking sheet and bake for 12 minutes, until the tops are golden and slightly crunchy.
Remove from oven, let rest 3 minutes, then gently fluff the couscous with a fork before serving.
Serve immediately to enjoy the buttery, crumbly texture and the aromatic char-grilled chillies.
